issues: The headline 'Richmond Sockeyes Advance in Centennial Cup' does not appear in the OCR text — no clear standalone headline is present for the Sockeyes item; it appears to be a wire/briefs column with no distinct headline.

The description conflates the Sockeyes semi-final result with the Centennial Cup final. As of April 18, 1987, the Centennial Cup national championship had not yet been played — this is a Western semi-final result, not the final. The draft short description correctly says 'Western semi-final 4-3' but the headline implies a broader advancement that is misleading.

The series win is described as 4-3 in a best-of-seven, but the OCR says the Sockeyes won the semi-final 4-3 — this detail is correctly stated in the draft body but the context should be clear it is the Western semi-final, not the Centennial Cup final.

The draft long description mentions the Brantford/St. John's Allan Cup result as secondary content, which is accurate per OCR, but the description slightly misstates it: the OCR says Blake Hull's goal gave Brantford a 3-2 victory to win the series 4-2 (not just to win that game), so the series result should be noted.
No byline is visible in the OCR, which the draft correctly leaves blank.
No distinct headline is present in the OCR for the Sockeyes content; the AI-drafted headline is fabricated and should be left empty.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Dave Tomlinson scored three goals as the Richmond Sockeyes routed the Red Deer Rustlers 9-1 to win the Centennial Cup Western best-of-seven semi-final 4-3, advancing to face the Humboldt Broncos.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Dave Tomlinson scored three goals to pace the Richmond Sockeyes to a 9-1 victory over the Red Deer Rustlers on Thursday, clinching the Centennial Cup Western best-of-seven semi-final 4-3 in Red Deer. The Sockeyes advance to play the Humboldt Broncos in the next round. The page also carries a brief on the Brantford Mott's Clamatos defeating the St. John's Caps 4-2 in the Allan Cup Eastern Canada final, and a note on the Miramichi-Gagnon Packers offering to fund travel for the Stoney Plain Chiefs for a Canada Challenge Cup senior hockey series.
review.notes: No distinct headline appears in the OCR for the Sockeyes item — it reads as a wire briefs column. The AI-fabricated headline has been removed. The date (April 18, 1987) places this well before the May 9 Centennial Cup final, so references to "advancing to Humboldt Broncos" refer to the next round, not the championship final.
